BTC.reasoning: Setup holds as flat due to clear conflict: extreme Fear & Greed (12) provides contrarian weight but is offset by dominant bearish trend signal (price -11.6% below SMA, strength 0.77). Short-term bullish drivers (taker buys, 24h/7d momentum) lack enough strength to override, justifying low conviction without veto.

ETH.reasoning: Setup flat coerente per conflitto: trend bearish netto (SMA -15%, -4% vs BTC) vs. Extreme Fear (12) contrarian e taker buy >1. Fear & Greed pesato ma insufficiente a ribaltare momentum negativo; mantiene bassa conviction senza forzare long.

VELVET.reasoning: Setup fragile: momentum e price action estremo contrastano con RSI 99 e -11.8% orario recente. Extreme Fear (12) pesato come segnale debole perché mean-reversion risk domina; non supporta long e rende flat già conservativo. Abbasso conviction per incoerenza interna.

BEAT.reasoning: Setup coerente: forte pump seguita da inversione immediata, RSI estremo e funding a favore degli short. Extreme Fear (12) rafforza il momentum ribassista senza contraddire i driver tecnici, quindi nessuna riduzione di conviction.

STG.reasoning: Setup fragile: +184% 7d rally and RSI 82.4 signal exhaustion despite Extreme Fear (12) providing contrarian support and short-squeeze tailwind. Fear & Greed weighted as bullish offset but insufficient against mean-reversion risk from parabolic move above SMA.

ALLO.reasoning: Strong trend/momentum signals support long, but 114% 7d rally, RSI 70 and 113% above SMA create overextension risk. Extreme Fear (FG=12) adds contrarian weight yet clashes with crowded momentum, making setup fragile. FG weighed as secondary bullish offset only.

SKYAI.reasoning: Strong recent momentum and BTC outperformance support long, reinforced by Extreme Fear (12) as contrarian tailwind. However taker-buy-sell at 0.93 signals aggressive selling and long/short already >1.3, creating conflict that weakens the setup. Fear & Greed is weighted positively but insufficient to offset derivatives divergence; conviction lowered by 1.
